text.title: Keyword Workflow: Build and Weekly Harvest
text.summary: Two operational loops: a one-shot build workflow for new ad groups and a weekly Search Terms harvest loop with agent-specific constraints.

section.title: Build Workflow
section.p: One-shot per ad group. Inputs: seed keywords, landing page URL, competitor list. Steps: expand via Keyword Planner + autocomplete, cluster by intent theme, assign match types (phrase-first), draft negative list. Output: ad group plan ready for upload.

rule.statement: Validate all agent-generated keywords against Keyword Planner volume before publishing.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent keywords that sound plausible ("ai-driven enterprise solution platform") but have zero search volume. Publishing them wastes Quality Score history slots and dilutes ad group relevance.

rule.statement: Cap agent bulk uploads at 50 keyword changes per push; require human approval before larger changes.
rule.rationale: A runaway agent loop can push 5,000 negatives in one session and gut a campaign. Human checkpoints are required before any push that changes more than 50 keywords or negatives.

section.title: Weekly Harvest Loop
section.p: Run weekly. Pull Search Terms report for the last 7-14 days. Classify each query with material spend: KEEP_AS_EXACT (relevant, converting), ADD_NEGATIVE_PHRASE (irrelevant theme), ADD_NEGATIVE_EXACT (specific irrelevant query), NEW_THEME (relevant but no ad group yet). Output as a diff for human review before applying.
section.reference: Google Ads API script to pull Search Terms data for classification

rule.statement: Never auto-apply Search Terms harvest results without human sign-off.
rule.rationale: A single misclassified negative can block an entire keyword theme from serving. The cost of a human review is a fraction of the cost of a week of blocked traffic.

antipattern.description: Agent promotes a query to exact match without checking for duplicates across ad groups.
antipattern.reason: The same keyword exact-matched in two ad groups creates internal competition — both ad groups bid on the same auction, raising your own CPC. Deduplicate account-wide before promoting.

antipattern.description: Agent uses exact-string matching to classify close-variant queries as negatives.
antipattern.reason: Google matches "running shoes" to "shoes for running" by default. String-matching misses close variants and prunes valid queries. Use semantic classification, not substring checks.

list.item: Require Keyword Planner volume validation before any new keyword is added.
Flag INVALID_KEYWORD_TEXT errors on long-tail keywords with punctuation — strip to letters/digits/spaces before submit.
Match keyword theme to landing page; mismatch is the second-largest Quality Score driver after expected CTR.
Keep one campaign per language and one per major geo — cross-language keyword pollution is the top cause of low Quality Score.
